vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on Backup group File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/group-, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0644 /etc/group-
vulnerability.group: Verify Permissions on Files with Local Account Information and Credentials

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only changes the mode of a backup copy of /etc/group. Adjusting permissions to 0644 follows standard STIG guidance, does not affect running services, authentication mechanisms, or networking, and is unlikely to cause system lockout or breakage.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Incorrect permissions could expose group information; Improper chmod syntax could leave the file insecure

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to follow workflow: PLAN then review_plan, then APPLY after approval. So first produce plan description. Need to modify permissions of backup file /etc/group- to 0644. Ensure using chmod. Use run_cmd to chmod 0644 /etc/group-. Also check existing mode maybe. Use read_file? Not needed for permissions, but maybe read file to ensure exists. We'll read /etc/group- to verify existence. Plan: read file, then run chmod. Use one command. Provide plan description.

Proceed.
